user_query:
Seed a local learning log for our engineering team from the provided incident feed and best-practices notes, then export, search, and summarize the results.

Requirements (read all files from input/ and write all outputs under output/):
1) Ingest incidents
- Read input/incidents.jsonl (each line is a JSON object) and record each entry as an error in a local learning log that persists to JSON on disk (no external services). Include attempted_solution, solution, and prevention_tip when present.
- Mark errors with a solution as resolved; leave others unresolved.
- When an error has a solution, also record a corresponding bug-fix learning that captures the fix (e.g., "Fixed: <error> → <solution>") so it’s searchable later.

2) Ingest learnings
- Read input/learnings_notes.md and record each learning with an appropriate category (technique, bug-fix, api-endpoint, constraint, best-practice, or error-handling). Preserve concise content and any useful context.

3) Run searches
- Read input/search_queries.txt (one query per line), run a keyword search over stored learnings for each query, and write a combined JSON file mapping each query string to an array of matching learnings (include at least id, category, content for each match) at output/search_results.json.

4) Summaries and exports
- Export the full dataset (all learnings and errors) to output/learnings_export.json.
- Generate a machine-readable summary with totals and category breakdowns at output/learning_summary.json.
- Generate a readable summary text at output/learning_summary.txt (include total learnings, category counts, and error statistics).
- Create a concise playbook at output/learning_playbook.md, grouping the most useful learnings by category and ending with a "Next Steps" section of 3 concrete recommendations for the team.

Acceptance criteria
- At least 8 total learnings are stored (combined from notes and bug-fix learnings derived from resolved errors).
- At least 4 distinct learning categories are represented.
- At least 4 errors exist in the export with >=1 unresolved.
- There is at least 1 bug-fix learning whose content begins with "Fixed:" or "Resolved:".
- output/search_results.json contains entries for all queries in input/search_queries.txt, and the queries "JSON" and "timeout" each have at least one result.
- The playbook contains at least two category sections (as markdown headings) and a "Next Steps" section.

Please keep everything local/offline. If anything in the files is ambiguous, make a reasonable categorization choice and document it briefly in the playbook.
